parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-565 (Risk B, 40 units, RP18.P05.M02.S15-RP18.P05.M04.S12, descriptor layer). The pack-level claim is honest: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review, with production_ready_flag scoped to descriptor verification only. Implementation is internally consistent across all eight refs: registry.js CP565 binding/no-write-attestation/requirements match the baseline, manifest, and plan (deliverable counts test:6/hermes_evidence:2/implementation:13/fixture:13/claude_review:2/security_audit:4=40; micro-phase M02:6/M03:22/M04:12; range and handoff boundaries align with CP564->CP565->CP566). service.js descriptor/case-set produce descriptor-only rows (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) and freezeCp565Result stamps a comprehensive no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime/no-leak attestation. validators.js coverage+slice validators enforce per-row guards, the no-write attestation, and the authority boundary; all 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js. The contract projection (p05_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor, cp565_requirements, cp565_no_write_attestation, current_pack) mirrors the live descriptor, and the validator script asserts deepEqual between contract and descriptor; command-evidence.json records the full validation suite (including rp18:ai-legal-workflows-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, git diff --check) at exit_code 0. Boundaries hold: Claude is read_only and explicitly not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic review/evidence descriptor packets (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No AI legal workflows/precedent/clause-markup/DD-extraction runtime is opened. No auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence is relied upon (readiness auth preflight passed). No P0/P1/P2 defects found. One P3 informational note: the manifest lists post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) not yet present, which is the correct pre-review state and is gated to be produced before commit. This is an independent reviewer recommendation, not final approval; human final approval remains required.

findings.title: Manifest references post-review closeout artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-565/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in the pack directory (confirmed via Glob). This is consistent with pack_level_claude_review.status='pending', empty valid_review_receipts, and command-evidence.json's note that these post-review gated artifacts/commands are produced from the accepted review before commit. It is not a defect and confirms the post-review closeout gating is wired correctly.
findings.recommendation: Proceed. After this accepted review, generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, then run the post-review gated validation commands (closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack-plan:generate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) before committing CP00-565.
